note: Enhancement amplitude-ring layer fails (enh_ber 0.11-0.29, model_net 0) -- the channel amplitude jitter (~0.19 median / 0.5 p90 log-mag) is comparable to the full ring step, so the two ring classes are not separable. Base DQPSK floor holds (base_model_net 5597-6082; >=5921 at eps<=0.316). Downside capped at base; NO upside from the enhancement. Combined-stream scoring (single RS over base+enh) is the WRONG model for a layered scheme and collapses to 2221-5597; the layered (separate-FEC) net = base+enh is the honest number and == base alone since enh contributes 0.

pure_base_baseline_same_seeds.note: pure proven r8 base (no enhancement) on the SAME harness seeds/payload -- the apples-to-apples baseline

honest_conclusion: Layered net == base net (enhancement contributes 0 recoverable bps at every power level). On identical seeds the PURE r8 base scores model_net 6471; the superposition scheme tops out at 6082 -- i.e. the enhancement perturbation slightly DEGRADES the base (more amplitude variation -> marginally worse DQPSK phase margin) and adds nothing. The amplitude-ring layer is killed by the channel amplitude jitter: log-mag jitter ~0.19 median / 0.50 p90 per symbol is comparable to the FULL ring step (log_switch 0.85 at the largest -8dB ring), so the stay/toggle classes are inseparable (enh_ber 0.11 at -8dB rising to 0.29 at -14dB; enh model_net = 0 throughout). The capped-downside property holds but the base rung already provides it for free, so the layered scheme is dominated by the plain base.
